twitter_thread: 27% of software stocks have lost over 50% of their value in the last year.
Atlassian's bottom-up sales approach has driven 30% YoY revenue growth.
Zoom's self-service model has cut CAC by 25%, outpacing industry averages.
Salesforce's over-reliance on outbound sales has led to a 15% decline in profitability.
A 10% increase in revenue retention can boost stock price by 20%.
What's the most important metric you track when evaluating software stocks? #softwarestocks #finance
pack.linkedin: The software stock market has seen 27% of stocks lose over 50% of their value in the last year. Atlassian's focus on bottom-up sales has driven 30% YoY revenue growth, while Zoom's self-service model has cut CAC by 25%. In contrast, Salesforce's outbound sales strategy has led to a 15% decline in profitability. A 10% increase in revenue retention can significantly boost stock price. What metric do you prioritize when evaluating software stocks?

pack.reddit_headline: Software stocks: are bottom-up sales the new path to profitability?
